# TTP — Text-To-Picture renderer

> A zero-dependency, static, frontend-only renderer that turns text into a
> 512x512 transparent WhatsApp-sticker image. No backend, no build step. A bot
> (any language) opens `render.html` with a headless browser (Puppeteer /
> Playwright), waits for a ready signal, and screenshots one DOM element.

## What this is

- Pure static files. Serve the folder from any host (nginx, GitHub Pages, S3,
  `python -m http.server`). There is no API server and no image is produced by
  this code — the integrator captures the screenshot.
- Font fitting runs entirely via canvas measurement (the `@chenglou/pretext`
  library, imported from `https://esm.sh/@chenglou/pretext`). The largest font
  size that fits the 512x512 box is found with a binary search; the result is
  written to the DOM exactly once. No DOM trial-and-error, no timers.

## Render contract

Open `render.html` with query parameters:

- `text` — the text to render (used by every mode except `both`). URL-encode
  it; `%0A` becomes a literal newline (line breaks are preserved).
- `mode` (optional, default `center`) — layout. One of:
    - `center` — one block, centered in the full 512x512 box.
    - `top` — one block, in the top third (caption).
    - `bottom` — one block, in the bottom third (the old "subtitle").
    - `both` — two blocks; reads `topText` and `bottomText` (NOT `text`). Both
      use a single shared font size.
    - `split` — `text` is auto-split into two halves at the word boundary
      nearest the visual midpoint; rendered top + bottom at one shared size.
  Any unrecognized value falls back to `center`.
- `topText`, `bottomText` — the two texts for `mode=both` only.
- `color` (optional, default `#ffffff`) — fill color (any CSS color).
- `strokeColor` (optional, default `#000000`) — outline color (any CSS color).
- `variant` (optional, default `ttp1`) — visual style, orthogonal to `mode`.
  Only `ttp1` (solid fill + outline, no background image) exists today. Any
  other value sets `data-error` on `#ttp` and does not render.

Examples:

    render.html?text=Hello%20World&color=%23ffcc00&strokeColor=%23000000
    render.html?mode=bottom&text=em%20manuten%C3%A7%C3%A3o
    render.html?mode=both&topText=one%20does%20not&bottomText=simply%20walk
    render.html?mode=split&text=olha%20a%20velocidade%20disso

## Expected output / how to consume

- The page renders into a single element with `id="ttp"`.
- `#ttp` is exactly 512x512 px, transparent background, text centered, white
  (or `color`) fill with a black (or `strokeColor`) outline, Impact font with
  color-emoji fallback.
- When rendering is complete, the element gets the attribute
  `data-ready="true"`. Integrators MUST wait for this before screenshotting.
- On failure the element gets `data-error="<message>"` and is NOT marked ready.

Integration steps:

1. Navigate to `render.html?...`.
2. `waitForSelector('#ttp[data-ready="true"]')`.
3. Screenshot the `#ttp` element handle with `omitBackground: true` to keep the
   transparent background. The PNG is your sticker.

## Scope

- v1 ships a single visual variant (`ttp1`) and five layout modes (`center`,
  `top`, `bottom`, `both`, `split`). `variant` (visual style) and `mode`
  (layout) are independent axes.
- No client-side PNG export, no analytics, no external runtime dependencies
  besides the Pretext CDN import.

## Self-hosting notes

- Serve the entire folder as-is. The only network dependency at runtime is the
  Pretext ESM import from esm.sh.
- Recommended headers: long-lived immutable cache on `/fonts`, `ttp.js`,
  `ttp.css` (`Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`), and
  `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` so cross-origin headless browsers can load
  the assets.
